understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to get a pushable block onto a goal tile marked with '+' on the semantic grid. Each level required navigating obstacles and strategically positioning the selected piece to push blocks into goal positions. The game had multiple levels to complete, with each level having a limited step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement used MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN to shift the currently selected piece in grid coordinates (3 pixels per cell). CLICK with a target position switched control between selectable pieces on the board. Some objects were pushable blocks that would move with the selected piece if adjacent in the movement direction. Walls blocked movement and couldn't be pushed. The movement_blocked hints indicated if adjacent objects existed but didn't guarantee success. Despite having multiple selectable pieces and pushable blocks visible on the board, clicking on most pieces/blocks resulted in 'blocked or action has no movement effect', suggesting they were either not valid selectables or were in states that prevented interaction. The level ultimately couldn't be completed within the 50-step budget, possibly due to misunderstanding which objects were actually selectable or the required sequence to solve the puzzle.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to get a pushable block onto a goal tile marked with '+' on the semantic grid. Each level required navigating obstacles and strategically positioning the selected piece to push blocks into goal positions. The game had multiple levels to complete, with each level having a limited step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement used MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN to shift the currently selected piece in grid coordinates (3 pixels per cell). CLICK with a target position switched control between selectable pieces on the board. Some objects were pushable blocks that would move with the selected piece if adjacent in the movement direction. Walls blocked movement and couldn't be pushed. The movement_blocked hints indicated if adjacent objects existed but didn't guarantee success. Despite having multiple selectable pieces and pushable blocks visible on the board, clicking on most pieces/blocks resulted in 'blocked or action has no movement effect', suggesting they were either not valid selectables or were in states that prevented interaction. The level ultimately couldn't be completed within the 50-step budget, possibly due to misunderstanding which objects were actually selectable or the required sequence to solve the puzzle.
